text.text:
A fairly new ramen spot in the 626, located at the corner of W. Live Oak Ave. and S. Santa Anita Ave. There's a Paris Baguette next to it, and it's in a small strip mall with a few empty storefronts across from it. There is also a private parking lot for the plaza, which makes parking convenient.

I had been seeing a lot of posts about this place on Instagram and social media over the past few months, and I also noticed they were participating in DineLA. A few days ago, while searching for a new dinner spot in the SGV, I remembered this place and looked them up on Google.

On the evening of dinner, I arrived around 5:00 PM, and a few tables were already filled. I met up with my friend at 5:30 PM, and the server greeted us warmly and seated us in one of the cute dining booths. There was even a bell at the booth, which made it easy to get the server's attention if we needed help.

They offer several ramen options, all of which looked very appealing. They also have other items such as yakitori, popcorn chicken, desserts, drinks, and alcoholic beverages.

Both of us ordered the yin-yang ramen, which includes two of their signature ramen styles: one non-spicy and one spicy. The spicy one was not as mild as the server described, especially since I do not handle spicy food very well. Still, the noodles and broth were both very good. I also ordered the beef and scallop yakitori, a yuzu soda, and all three of their desserts.

Overall, the food was excellent. I would rate everything around an 85 to 90 out of 100, including the excellent service and creative interior design. They also have a boba shop on the right side of the restaurant when you walk in, which is a nice bonus.

text.text:
Overall, the experience was just okay and a bit underwhelming.

The biggest disappointment was the ramen itself. The flavor felt off compared to what I’m used to, and it didn’t really hit the mark. We also had an issue with customization, when we asked for the egg to be well done, the server refused and insisted it would cook in the broth, which it didn’t. It came out runny, which wasn’t what we wanted.

That said, the appetizers were a highlight. The crispy chili wings were excellent, definitely the best thing we ordered. The deep fried dumplings were also very good. The squid was decent but a bit bland. Overall, the food did seem fresh.

Service was average, but there was a frustrating miscommunication around the happy hour menu that felt a bit misleading and resulted in a higher than expected bill.

The atmosphere was easily the standout. The seating was unique we were placed in an elevated, honeycomb style area that gave a great view. The decor throughout the restaurant was impressive and added a lot to the experience. The drinks were also well done, and I liked that there’s a separate area where you can just stop in for a drink.

If I come back, it would likely be for the appetizers and drinks rather than the ramen, which is disappointing for a ramen spot.

text.text: The restaurant itself is really aesthetically pleasing.  Service was a little slow on the night friends and I dined in.  I did enjoy the omurice rice with curry.  It was delicious, but nothing out of this world.  Maybe I should give the ramen a try in the future.  The matcha tiramisu and sakura jelly were okay as well.  Overall a nice decent meal.

It was delicious, but nothing out of this world.  Maybe I should give the ramen a try in the future.  The matcha tiramisu and sakura jelly were okay as well.  Overall a nice decent meal.

text.text:
The atmosphere is really cool and the quality of the food matches! We got the app sampler, I got the katsuobushi pork ramen and my boyfriend got the shoyu beef ramen. We both loved ours and tried each other's broth - suuuper good.

My only complaint was the service was really good at the start, but after we got our food it went downhill from there. No refills, nobody checking in on us long after we finished our food. We wanted to order both desserts, but decided not to because we waited so long for anyone to come by our table. It was a little confusing if we had a specific server or just any server that happened to walk by.

Regardless of the service, we understood that it's right after the holidays and it was a pretty packed house. We'll still go again because the food and vibes are worth it and give the staff another chance.

text.text: This place got the vibe! The food isn’t the best but not bad either. If you want a great ramen (as the name of the place suggested)? it’s not the place. BUT their omu rice, takoyaki, and the rice burger ARE pretty delicious. AND its a heck of a place to hang out with friends or your date on the weekend. In short, everything but the ramen is YUMMMY!
